response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest (test runners, assertion libraries, IDE component work on LabWindows/CVI, MCP code generators), but the core of this role is deep Java/Kotlin and JVM expertise with Spring — and that is essentially absent. Java appears only as a listed skill with brief peripheral use, Kotlin and Spring do not appear at all, and his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Strong engineer, wrong stack for an enterprise JVM IDE role.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and language ecosystems (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, MCP code generators)
Prior IDE product experience contributing C/C++ components to NI LabWindows/CVI
Long track record across complex systems, from low-latency microservices to full-stack platforms
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge
Recent experience centers on TypeScript/Node.js product work, far from enterprise JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
